justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as CRITICAL with options and recommendation. Clock-start ambiguity identified in assumptions ('2-hour express window starts at order placement, not at driver pickup') and in edge cases (driver cancel at minute 115). Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-7 and in escalation #2. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict addressed via escalation #2 (auto-refund vs wrong-address exemption collision). 9 business rules extracted with correct invariant classifications. 2 formal escalations with detailed options and recommendations. Confidence level is 45, well within the <=50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction identified as CRITICAL escalation with three resolution options and a nuanced recommendation (Option C hybrid approach)

9 well-structured business rules with appropriate invariant classifications — BR-1 (10km) and BR-3 (15-min pickup) correctly marked as invariants, pricing and policy rules correctly marked as non-invariant
Clock-start ambiguity captured in assumptions section and implicitly in edge cases, though not elevated to a formal escalation
Wrong-address exemption thoroughly covered as BR-7 and surfaced as a collision point with auto-refund in escalation #2
Excellent edge cases including the 10km boundary inclusivity question, driver cancel at minute 115, multiple driver cancellations, and warehouse hours gap
Confidence at 45 with clear reasoning about why the contradictions prevent finalization
Clock-start ambiguity (PM implies from order placement, Ops implies from pickup) not elevated to a formal escalation — it appears only as an assumption. The rubric expected this as an identified gap.

The 3-business-day processing vs auto-refund timing tension is not directly called out — escalation #2 focuses on the wrong-address collision rather than the processing delay conflict with immediate auto-refund
